channel.title: BIOS Confessions
channel.description: Clear, structured explanations of core CS topics, from fundamentals to advanced principles. Learn for free, at your own pace.

item.title: Why Build an OS? The Case for Going All the Way Down
item.description: Stop treating the OS as a black box. Discover why building an AArch64 kernel from scratch on ARM is the ultimate systems programming challenge for 2026. From silicon to shell, we go all the way down.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: From Silicon to Shell: Building an OS for ARM and More
item.description: A bare-metal journey from a powered-on AArch64 CPU to a working interactive shell. Thirteen chapters of writing a kernel from scratch — bootloader, MMU, interrupts, allocator, processes, syscalls, filesystem, and a Rust no_std module — all running on QEMU's virt machine.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: Itô's Lemma Explained: Stochastic Calculus for Finance
item.description: Why does classical calculus fail for random variables? Learn the intuition behind Itô's Lemma, its formal derivation, and how to solve Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) in Python.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Random Walk: From Coin Flips to Stochastic Calculus
item.description: Discover how a simple coin flip evolves into the mathematics powering modern derivative pricing. From Binomial Trees to the Wiener Process and the Heat Equation; we handle all in Python.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: Data Science with Python
item.description: From zero to insights. A comprehensive beginner's guide to setting up your environment, cleaning messy datasets, and uncovering stories through data visualization.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Finding P values and false hope: A Guide to Risk and Quant Finance
item.description: From simple bets to complex bond portfolios. Explore the mathematical structures of uncertainty, debt mechanics, and the Python tools used to model them.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Tough Love Architecture Guide
item.description: Stop pattern-worshipping and start solving problems. A cynical but practical journey through software design, incremental refactoring, and high-performance systems.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: Is Sweet Bonanza 1000 Rigged? A Statistical Analysis of 60,000 Spins
item.description: We analyze 60,000 spins of Sweet Bonanza 1000 using Python, Bootstrap CI, and Hypothesis Testing to see if the 96.53% RTP holds up against extreme volatility.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Value at Risk (VaR) vs. CVaR: A Practical Guide for Investors
item.description: Stop using abstract volatility. Learn how to calculate Value at Risk (VaR) and Conditional VaR (CVaR) using Python to understand your actual downside potential.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: A Python-Based Statistical Audit of Gates of Olympus
item.description: Using Python, statistics, and 40,000 spins to analyze RTP, variance, bonus frequency, and volatility in Gates of Olympus through hypothesis testing and simulation.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Stock Options 101: A Guide to Calls, Puts, and Pricing
item.description: Unlock the basics of stock options. Learn the difference between calls and puts, American vs. European styles, and how Greeks like Delta and Theta impact pricing.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Visual Storyteller: Mastering EDA with Seaborn and Python
item.description: Turn raw data into actionable insights. Learn how to use Seaborn and Matplotlib for Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA), including heatmaps, Q-Q plots, and distribution analysis.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Portfolio Allocation: The Math Behind the Efficient Frontier
item.description: Master the quantitative core of Modern Portfolio Theory. Learn how to use Markowitz optimization and Python to build an efficient frontier for asset allocation.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Cleaning Machine: 6 Steps to Trustworthy Data with Pandas
item.description: Master the 6-step framework for data wrangling. Learn to handle missing values, remove outliers using IQR, and validate data quality using Python and Pandas.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Stock Analysis with Python: Modeling Prices, Returns & Distributions
item.description: Learn how to analyze stock market data using Python. This guide covers calculating daily returns, visualizing volatility, and modeling statistical distributions with yfinance.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Lab Setup: Preparing Your Local and Cloud Python Environment
item.description: Master your Data Science workflow. A step-by-step tutorial on setting up VS Code, Python virtual environments (venv), and Google Colab for professional analysis.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: The Geometry of Interest: Constructing Yield Curves and Forward Rates
item.description: Master the term structure of interest rates. Learn to build yield curves using bootstrapping and interpolation, and derive forward rates with practical Python examples.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: Beyond the CPU: I/O, Buses, and Multicore Systems
item.description: Discover how CPUs talk to the outside world. Learn about memory-mapped I/O, DMA, the role of GPUs in parallel processing, and modern System-on-Chip (SoC) design.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Life in the Tranches: Understanding CDOs and Risk Waterfalls
item.description: Learn how CDO tranching splits credit risk into Senior, Mezzanine, and Equity layers — with a step-by-step Python waterfall simulation.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The CPU in Action: Mastering the Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle
item.description: The grand finale of our architecture series. Follow a step-by-step walkthrough of the Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle to see how the ALU, Control Unit, and Registers create a thinking machine.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Loans and Bonds: The Mechanics of Borrowing and Default Risk
item.description: A quantitative guide to loan amortization and bond pricing. Learn to derive payment formulas, simulate default risk, and understand coupon rates using Python.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Memory Hierarchy: From CPU Registers to Long-Term Storage
item.description: Master the computer memory hierarchy. Learn the critical differences between L1/L2/L3 cache, RAM, and SSDs, plus how the MMU handles virtual-to-physical address translation.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: The Art of Losing Slowly: Risk, Variance, and Decision-Making
item.description: Master risk management through the lens of variance and expected value. Learn how to size bets, diversify risk, and implement decision-making logic in Python.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: The Control Unit: The Brain Inside the CPU
item.description: Discover how the Control Unit (CU) orchestrates the CPU. Learn the mechanics of instruction decoding, hardwired vs. microprogrammed logic, and the role of microcode.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Expected Riches and Expensive Lessons: A Data Science Audit of Modern Slots
item.description: Moving beyond 'luck.' Using Python, Monte Carlo simulations, and hypothesis testing to dissect the mathematical reality of high-volatility gambling.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: How an ALU Works: The Heart of CPU Architecture Explained
item.description: Don't just read about the ALU, learn how to build one. Let's break down the Arithmetic Logic Unit into 1-bit circuits using adders, multiplexers, and logic gates. Perfect for students of computer architecture.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: Design Patterns for Performance: Scaling Beyond Simple Code
item.description: Master high-performance architecture. Explore the Reactor pattern, Actor model, and resilience strategies like Circuit Breakers and Load Shedding for low-latency systems.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: x86-64 Assembly Hello World: The Complete Docker-Based Guide
item.description: Stop fighting environment errors. Learn x86-64 Assembly with a zero-setup Docker environment. Includes NASM code, syscall explanations, and a one-click build script.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: The Rescue Mission: Refactoring a 'Monster' Class Step-by-Step
item.description: Don't delete that messy code just yet. Learn how to transform a tightly coupled Java class into a clean, testable architecture using incremental refactoring and abstractions.
item.author: Nathan

item.title: Logic Gates & Memory: Building an 8-Bit Adder from Scratch
item.description: Master the building blocks of digital logic. Learn how transistors form AND/OR gates, how Full-Adders perform binary arithmetic, and how Flip-Flops create computer memory.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: How Transistors Work: From Semiconductors to MOSFETs
item.description: A deep dive into the world of transistors. Learn about semiconductor physics, P-type vs N-type doping, and how MOSFETs function as the building blocks of CPUs.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: From Transistor to System: A Friendly Guide to Computer Architecture
item.description: Demystifying the ghost in the machine. A journey from raw silicon and jumping electrons to the sophisticated multicore systems that power our modern world.
item.author: Gianni

item.title: The Art of Saying No: The Harsh Truth About Design Patterns
item.description: Stop pattern-worshipping and start solving problems. Learn why most developers misuse design patterns and how to pick the right architecture for real-world constraints.
item.author: Nathan
